2606.16100.title: Your "Pro" LLM Subscription May Actually Be "Free": Exposing Fingerprint Spoofing Risks in LLM Inference Services
2606.16100.paper_summary: As Large Language Model (LLM) APIs become ubiquitous, users increasingly rely on black-box fingerprinting to verify that providers are serving the advertised premium models. However, these methods may overlook adversarial providers who manipulate model weights to cheat the fingerprint process. We introduce a novel threat termed fingerprint spoofing, where a malicious provider stealthily serves a weaker model that has been parameter-efficiently fine-tuned to mimic a stronger model, thereby evading user-side fingerprinting. We first formally prove that user-side resource constraints (i.e., finite query budgets and weak fingerprinting classifiers) make current fingerprinting vulnerable to fingerprint spoofing. Guided by this theoretical analysis, we propose GhostPrint, a cost-effective attack framework leveraging surrogate modeling, reward-ranked fine-tuning, and knowledge distillation. Extensive evaluations in both static and continual fingerprinting settings demonstrate that GhostPrint allows weak models to consistently bypass representative fingerprint methods while maintaining utility at a low fine-tuning cost, exposing a critical vulnerability in current LLM fingerprinting pipelines.
2606.16100.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLM）API的普及，用戶越來越依賴黑箱指紋識別來驗證提供者是否提供宣傳的高級模型。<lb/>然而，這些方法可能忽略那些操縱模型權重以作弊指紋過程的對抗性提供者。<lb/>我們引入了一種新威脅，稱為指紋欺騙，其中一個惡意提供者悄悄地提供了一個經過參數高效微調以模仿更強模型的較弱模型，從而逃避用戶端的指紋識別。<lb/>我們首先正式證明，用戶端的資源限制（即有限的查詢預算和弱指紋分類器）使當前的指紋識別容易受到指紋欺騙的攻擊。<lb/>在這一理論分析的指導下，我們提出了GhostPrint，一個利用代理建模、獎勵排名微調和知識蒸餾的成本效益攻擊框架。<lb/>在靜態和持續指紋識別設置中的廣泛評估表明，GhostPrint使得弱模型能夠持續繞過代表性的指紋方法，同時在低微調成本下保持效用，揭示了當前LLM指紋識別管道中的一個關鍵漏洞。
2606.16100.author: Jiahao Zhang et.al.
2606.16100.authors: Jiahao Zhang, Xiuyu Li, Suhang Wang
